revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

{{神社| 名称=春日神社| 所在地=大分県大分市勢家町4丁目6-87| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|42|N|131|35|53.6|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=武甕槌命
経津主命
天津児屋根命
姫大神| 社格=県社・別表神社| 創建=[[貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）| 例祭=[[4月13日]]| 神事=潮掻神事}} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[大分市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 [[春日神]]（[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]]・[[経津主神|経津主命]]・[[天児屋命|天津児屋根命]]・[[比売神|姫大神]]）を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）、[[豊後国]][[国司]]となった藤原世数により[[氏神]]である奈良[[春日大社]]を勧請して創建された。[[天平]]年間（[[729年]] - [[749年]]）、石川年足が大和[[若草山|三笠山]]より勧請したとも伝えられる。[[鎌倉時代]]の[[建久]]7年、豊後国の[[地頭]]となった[[大友能直]]が再興し、以来、代々の領主により府内の総廟として崇敬された。明治4年に県社に列格した。 [[天正]]12年（[[1584年]]）の島津軍の入府の際に、[[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）には米軍の空襲により社殿を焼失した。現在の社殿は昭和42年（[[1967年]]）に再建されたものである。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kasuganomori.jp 春日神社]（公式サイト） [[Category:大分県の神社|かすかしんしや おおいたし]] [[Category:府県社|かすかしんしや おおいたし]] [[Category:別表神社|かすかしんしや おおいたし]] [[Category:春日神社|かすかしんしや おおいたし]] {{shinto-stub}}

姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝貞観2年（860年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月13日]] |神事 = 潮掻き神事（10月19日） }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[大分市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]]、[[経津主神|経津主命]]、[[天児屋命|天津児屋根（あまつこやね）命）]]、'''姫大神'''（ひめおおかみ）の[[春日四神]]を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）、[[豊後国]][[国司]]となった[[藤原世数]]により[[氏神]]である[[大和国]]の[[春日大社]]を[[勧請]]して創建された。[[天平]]年間（[[729年]] - [[749年]]）、[[石川年足]]が大和[[春日山 (奈良県)|三笠山]]より勧請したとも伝えられる。[[鎌倉時代]]の[[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]）、豊後国の[[地頭]]となった[[大友能直]]が再興し、以来、代々の領主により[[府内 (豊後国)|豊後府内]]の総廟として崇敬された。 [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）県社に昇格した。 == 社殿 == [[天正]]12年（[[1584年]]）の島津軍の入府の際に、[[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）には米軍の空襲により社殿を焼失した。現在の社殿は昭和42年（[[1967年]]）に再建されたものである。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kasuganomori.jp 春日神社]（神社公式） {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや}} [[Category:大分市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 春日神社 |画像 = [[File:Oita kasuga jinjya.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 大分県大分市勢家町4丁目6-87 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 武甕槌命

姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝貞観2年（860年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月13日]]{{cite web |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/ |title=恒例祭 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }} |神事 = 茅の輪神事（6月30日）
潮掻き神事（10月19日） }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[大分市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]]（たけみかづちのみこと）、[[経津主神|経津主命]]（ふつぬしのみこと）、[[天児屋命|天津児屋根命]]（あまつこやねのみこと）、[[比売神|姫大神]]（ひめおおかみ）の[[春日神|春日四神]]を祀る{{cite web |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/ |title=由緒 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝や『[[豊後国志]]』によれば、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）、[[豊後国]][[国司]]となった[[藤原世数]]により[[氏神]]である[[大和国]]の[[春日大社]]を[[勧請]]して創建された{{cite encyclopedia |title=春日神社 |author=吉田豊治 |encyclopedia=大分百科事典 |page=137 |editor=大分放送大分百科事典刊行本部 |publisher=大分放送 |date=1980-12-01 }}。『春日神社略記』では、[[天平]]年間（[[729年]] - [[749年]]）、[[国分寺]]建立のために下向した[[石川年足]]が大和[[春日山 (奈良県)|三笠山]]より勧請したとする。また、[[天長]]年間（[[824年]] - [[834年]]）、市川某が大和三笠山より勧請したともされる{{cite web |url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html |title=勢家 春日神社 |publisher=全国春日連合会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 === 中近世 === [[鎌倉時代]]の[[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]）、豊後国[[守護]]となった[[大友氏]]初代当主[[大友能直]]が再興したとされる。一方、『雉城雑誌』は、第2代当主[[大友親秀]]が[[仁治]]3年（[[1243年]]）に社殿を修復して笠和郷[[大分郡]]に10あった[[郷#日本における郷|郷]]のひとつ。の宗廟とし、社領80貫を寄進したと記している。親秀が寄進した菖蒲田等の社領は、近年まで勢家町及び春日町の小字に名を残していた{{Cite book |和書 |title=デジタルブック版「大分今昔」第十五章 春日浦かいわい |author=渡辺克己 |editor=大分合同新聞社 |publisher=NAN-NAN事務局 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf |format=PDF |date=2007-11-23 }}。親秀はまた、[[別当寺]]である鷹雄山神宮寺を再興したり、境内に蓬莱山を築いたともされる{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7 |title=特別保護樹林一覧 |publisher=大分県 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。以来、代々の領主により[[府内 (豊後国)|豊後府内]]の総廟として崇敬された。 [[天正]]14年（[[1586年]]）の[[豊薩合戦|島津軍の府内侵攻]]の際に社殿を焼失。[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）に[[府内藩]]初代藩主[[竹中重利]]が再興した。重利は、慶長12年（[[1607年]]）、[[江戸]]からの帰路に[[播磨灘]]で暴風雨に遭ったが、春日神社に加護を祈って事なきを得たので、境内に松の苗10万本を寄進したという。 [[日根野吉明]]は、[[寛永]]11年（[[1634年]]）に府内藩に入ると、春日神社に神宝を寄進するとともに、伝記を蒐集して祭祀を復興。[[正保]]2年（[[1645年]]）には蓬莱山を再建するとともに、[[過去七仏]]の廟を建立した。 === 近現代 === [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]に列格し、[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に県社に昇格した{{cite web |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf |title=「大分きれい100選事業」受賞作品【建築物・工作物・まちづくり活動部門】平成24年度おおいた景観モデル賞 48.春日神社 |publisher=大分市 |date=2017-04-01 |format=PDF |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 [[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）[[7月17日]]未明の[[大分空襲]]では米軍の[[焼夷弾]]により社殿を焼失。[[第二次世界大戦]]後は、[[神道指令]]により[[八幡朝見神社]]に合祀された[[別府市]]青山町の[[温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|温泉神社]]の社殿を移築し、仮殿とした{{cite news |url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337 |title=温泉神社の意外な事実 かつての社殿は春日神社に |newspaper=大分合同新聞 |date=2018-07-23 }}。 現在の社殿は昭和42年（[[1967年]]）に再建されたもので、同年[[10月18日]]に遷座祭が斎行された。なお、社殿の再建後、旧温泉神社の社殿は神輿を納める蔵として使用されている。 昭和43年（[[1968年]]）7月には神社本庁より別表神社に列せられている。 == 社叢 == 社叢は、総面積 26,000m2、樹林面積 15,600m2で、樹木の総数は約500本。「春日神社の森」として、[[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[2月1日]]に大分市の名木（樹林の部）{{cite web |url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html |title=大分市指定名木一覧表 |publisher=大分市 |date=2016-09-14 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}に、また、同年[[3月15日]]に大分県の特別保護樹林に指定されている。 == 祭礼 == * [[1月1日]] - [[歳旦祭]]・[[初詣]] * [[初午|2月初午]] - 摂社稲荷神社初午祭 * [[2月11日]] - [[紀元節|紀元祭]] * [[2月17日]] - [[祈年祭]] * [[4月12日]] - 春季大祭宵祭 * [[4月13日]] - 例祭 * [[4月29日]] - 昭和祭 * [[5月1日]] - 摂社金比羅社祭 * [[6月30日]] - 夏越[[大祓|大祓式]]・[[大祓#茅の輪くぐり|茅の輪神事]] * [[7月18日]] - 夏季大祭宵祭 * [[7月19日]] - 夏越大祭・神輿巡行 * [[9月25日]] - 摂社天満社祭 * [[10月18日]] - 秋季大祭宵祭 * [[10月19日]] - 秋季大祭・潮掻き神事 * [[11月15日]] - [[七五三|七五三祭]] * [[11月23日]] - [[新嘗祭]] * [[12月23日]] - [[天皇誕生日|天長祭]] * [[12月31日]] - 師走大祓式・除夜祭 == 交通 == * 車 - [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[大分駅]]から約5分。 * バス ** 大分駅前7番のりばから、[[大分県立図書館|県立図書館]]行きで「春日神社前」停留所下車。 ** 大分駅前2番のりばから、別府・国東方面行きで「春日浦」停留所下車、徒歩3-4分{{cite web |url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143 |title=春日神社 |publisher=一般財団法人大分市観光協会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp 春日神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} |date=2016-09-14 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}に、また、同年[[3月15日]]に大分県の特別保護樹林に指定されている。 == 祭礼 == * [[1月1日]] - [[歳旦祭]]・[[初詣]] * [[初午|2月初午]] - 摂社稲荷神社初午祭 * [[2月11日]] - [[紀元節|紀元祭]] * [[2月17日]] - [[祈年祭]] * [[4月12日]] - 春季大祭宵祭 * [[4月13日]] - 例祭 * [[4月29日]] - 昭和祭 * [[5月1日]] - 摂社金比羅社祭 * [[6月30日]] - 夏越[[大祓|大祓式]]・[[大祓#茅の輪くぐり|茅の輪神事]] * [[7月18日]] - 夏季大祭宵祭 * [[7月19日]] - 夏越大祭・神輿巡行 * [[9月25日]] - 摂社天満社祭 * [[10月18日]] - 秋季大祭宵祭 * [[10月19日]] - 秋季大祭・潮掻き神事 * [[11月15日]] - [[七五三|七五三祭]] * [[11月23日]] - [[新嘗祭]] * [[12月23日]] - [[天皇誕生日|天長祭]] * [[12月31日]] - 師走大祓式・除夜祭 == 交通 == * 車 - [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[大分駅]]から約5分。 * バス ** 大分駅前7番のりばから、[[大分県立図書館|県立図書館]]行きで「春日神社前」停留所下車。 ** 大分駅前2番のりばから、別府・国東方面行きで「春日浦」停留所下車、徒歩3-4分{{cite web |url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143 |title=春日神社 |publisher=一般財団法人大分市観光協会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp 春日神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや おおいたし}} revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 春日神社 |画像 = [[File:Oita kasuga jinjya.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 大分県大分市勢家町4丁目6-87 |位置 = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 武甕槌命
経津主命
天津児屋根命
姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝貞観2年（860年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月13日]]{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/ |title=恒例祭 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }} |神事 = 茅の輪神事（6月30日）
潮掻き神事（10月19日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=春日神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[大分市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]]（たけみかづちのみこと）、[[経津主神|経津主命]]（ふつぬしのみこと）、[[天児屋命|天津児屋根命]]（あまつこやねのみこと）、[[比売神|姫大神]]（ひめおおかみ）の[[春日神|春日四神]]を祀る{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/ |title=由緒 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝や『[[豊後国志]]』によれば、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）、[[豊後国]][[国司]]となった[[藤原世数]]により[[氏神]]である[[大和国]]の[[春日大社]]を[[勧請]]して創建された{{cite encyclopedia |title=春日神社 |author=吉田豊治 |encyclopedia=大分百科事典 |page=137 |editor=大分放送大分百科事典刊行本部 |publisher=大分放送 |date=1980-12-01 }}。『春日神社略記』では、[[天平]]年間（[[729年]] - [[749年]]）、[[国分寺]]建立のために下向した[[石川年足]]が大和[[春日山 (奈良県)|三笠山]]より勧請したとする。また、[[天長]]年間（[[824年]] - [[834年]]）、市川某が大和三笠山より勧請したともされる{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html |title=勢家 春日神社 |publisher=全国春日連合会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 === 中近世 === [[鎌倉時代]]の[[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]）、豊後国[[守護]]となった[[大友氏]]初代当主[[大友能直]]が再興したとされる。一方、『雉城雑誌』は、第2代当主[[大友親秀]]が[[仁治]]3年（[[1243年]]）に社殿を修復して笠和郷[[大分郡]]に10あった[[郷#日本における郷|郷]]のひとつ。の宗廟とし、社領80貫を寄進したと記している。親秀が寄進した菖蒲田等の社領は、近年まで勢家町及び春日町の小字に名を残していた{{Cite book |和書 |title=デジタルブック版「大分今昔」第十五章 春日浦かいわい |author=渡辺克己 |editor=大分合同新聞社 |publisher=NAN-NAN事務局 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf |format=PDF |date=2007-11-23 }}。親秀はまた、[[別当寺]]である鷹雄山神宮寺を再興したり、境内に蓬莱山を築いたともされる{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7 |title=特別保護樹林一覧 |publisher=大分県 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。以来、代々の領主により[[府内 (豊後国)|豊後府内]]の総廟として崇敬された。 [[天正]]14年（[[1586年]]）の[[豊薩合戦|島津軍の府内侵攻]]の際に社殿を焼失。[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）に[[府内藩]]初代藩主[[竹中重利]]が再興した。重利は、慶長12年（[[1607年]]）、[[江戸]]からの帰路に[[播磨灘]]で暴風雨に遭ったが、春日神社に加護を祈って事なきを得たので、境内に松の苗10万本を寄進したという。 [[日根野吉明]]は、[[寛永]]11年（[[1634年]]）に府内藩に入ると、春日神社に神宝を寄進するとともに、伝記を蒐集して祭祀を復興。[[正保]]2年（[[1645年]]）には蓬莱山を再建するとともに、[[過去七仏]]の廟を建立した。 === 近現代 === [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]に列格し、[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に県社に昇格した{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf |title=「大分きれい100選事業」受賞作品【建築物・工作物・まちづくり活動部門】平成24年度おおいた景観モデル賞 48.春日神社 |publisher=大分市 |date=2017-04-01 |format=PDF |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 [[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）[[7月17日]]未明の[[大分空襲]]では米軍の[[焼夷弾]]により社殿を焼失。[[第二次世界大戦]]後は、[[神道指令]]により[[八幡朝見神社]]に合祀された[[別府市]]青山町の[[温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|温泉神社]]の社殿を移築し、仮殿とした{{cite news |url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337 |title=温泉神社の意外な事実 かつての社殿は春日神社に |newspaper=大分合同新聞 |date=2018-07-23 }}。 現在の社殿は昭和42年（[[1967年]]）に再建されたもので、同年[[10月18日]]に遷座祭が斎行された。なお、社殿の再建後、旧温泉神社の社殿は神輿を納める蔵として使用されている。 昭和43年（[[1968年]]）7月には神社本庁より別表神社に列せられている。 == 社叢 == 社叢は、総面積 26,000m2、樹林面積 15,600m2で、樹木の総数は約500本。「春日神社の森」として、[[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[2月1日]]に大分市の名木（樹林の部）{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html |title=大分市指定名木一覧表 |publisher=大分市 |date=2016-09-14 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}に、また、同年[[3月15日]]に大分県の特別保護樹林に指定されている。 == 祭礼 == * [[1月1日]] - [[歳旦祭]]・[[初詣]] * [[初午|2月初午]] - 摂社稲荷神社初午祭 * [[2月11日]] - [[紀元節|紀元祭]] * [[2月17日]] - [[祈年祭]] * [[4月12日]] - 春季大祭宵祭 * [[4月13日]] - 例祭 * [[4月29日]] - 昭和祭 * [[5月1日]] - 摂社金比羅社祭 * [[6月30日]] - 夏越[[大祓|大祓式]]・[[大祓#茅の輪くぐり|茅の輪神事]] * [[7月18日]] - 夏季大祭宵祭 * [[7月19日]] - 夏越大祭・神輿巡行 * [[9月25日]] - 摂社天満社祭 * [[10月18日]] - 秋季大祭宵祭 * [[10月19日]] - 秋季大祭・潮掻き神事 * [[11月15日]] - [[七五三|七五三祭]] * [[11月23日]] - [[新嘗祭]] * [[12月23日]] - [[天皇誕生日|天長祭]] * [[12月31日]] - 師走大祓式・除夜祭 == 交通 == * 車 - [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[大分駅]]から約5分。 * バス ** 大分駅前7番のりばから、[[大分県立図書館|県立図書館]]行きで「春日神社前」停留所下車。 ** 大分駅前2番のりばから、別府・国東方面行きで「春日浦」停留所下車、徒歩3-4分{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143 |title=春日神社 |publisher=一般財団法人大分市観光協会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp 春日神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや おおいたし}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分市の建築物]] [[Category:大分市の歴史]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

revision.comment: 戻し：中途半端な状態のテンプレートであり、掲載不適当と判断。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 春日神社 |画像 = [[File:Oita kasuga jinjya.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 大分県大分市勢家町4丁目6-87 |位置 = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 武甕槌命
経津主命
天津児屋根命
姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝貞観2年（860年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月13日]]{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/ |title=恒例祭 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }} |神事 = 茅の輪神事（6月30日）
潮掻き神事（10月19日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=春日神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[大分市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]]（たけみかづちのみこと）、[[経津主神|経津主命]]（ふつぬしのみこと）、[[天児屋命|天津児屋根命]]（あまつこやねのみこと）、[[比売神|姫大神]]（ひめおおかみ）の[[春日神|春日四神]]を祀る{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/ |title=由緒 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝や『[[豊後国志]]』によれば、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）、[[豊後国]][[国司]]となった[[藤原世数]]により[[氏神]]である[[大和国]]の[[春日大社]]を[[勧請]]して創建された{{cite encyclopedia |title=春日神社 |author=吉田豊治 |encyclopedia=大分百科事典 |page=137 |editor=大分放送大分百科事典刊行本部 |publisher=大分放送 |date=1980-12-01 }}。『春日神社略記』では、[[天平]]年間（[[729年]] - [[749年]]）、[[国分寺]]建立のために下向した[[石川年足]]が大和[[春日山 (奈良県)|三笠山]]より勧請したとする。また、[[天長]]年間（[[824年]] - [[834年]]）、市川某が大和三笠山より勧請したともされる{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html |title=勢家 春日神社 |publisher=全国春日連合会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 === 中近世 === [[鎌倉時代]]の[[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]）、豊後国[[守護]]となった[[大友氏]]初代当主[[大友能直]]が再興したとされる。一方、『雉城雑誌』は、第2代当主[[大友親秀]]が[[仁治]]3年（[[1243年]]）に社殿を修復して笠和郷[[大分郡]]に10あった[[郷#日本における郷|郷]]のひとつ。の宗廟とし、社領80貫を寄進したと記している。親秀が寄進した菖蒲田等の社領は、近年まで勢家町及び春日町の小字に名を残していた{{Cite book |和書 |title=デジタルブック版「大分今昔」第十五章 春日浦かいわい |author=渡辺克己 |editor=大分合同新聞社 |publisher=NAN-NAN事務局 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf |format=PDF |date=2007-11-23 }}。親秀はまた、[[別当寺]]である鷹雄山神宮寺を再興したり、境内に蓬莱山を築いたともされる{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7 |title=特別保護樹林一覧 |publisher=大分県 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。以来、代々の領主により[[府内 (豊後国)|豊後府内]]の総廟として崇敬された。 [[天正]]14年（[[1586年]]）の[[豊薩合戦|島津軍の府内侵攻]]の際に社殿を焼失。[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）に[[府内藩]]初代藩主[[竹中重利]]が再興した。重利は、慶長12年（[[1607年]]）、[[江戸]]からの帰路に[[播磨灘]]で暴風雨に遭ったが、春日神社に加護を祈って事なきを得たので、境内に松の苗10万本を寄進したという。 [[日根野吉明]]は、[[寛永]]11年（[[1634年]]）に府内藩に入ると、春日神社に神宝を寄進するとともに、伝記を蒐集して祭祀を復興。[[正保]]2年（[[1645年]]）には蓬莱山を再建するとともに、[[過去七仏]]の廟を建立した。 === 近現代 === [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]に列格し、[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に県社に昇格した{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf |title=「大分きれい100選事業」受賞作品【建築物・工作物・まちづくり活動部門】平成24年度おおいた景観モデル賞 48.春日神社 |publisher=大分市 |date=2017-04-01 |format=PDF |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 [[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）[[7月17日]]未明の[[大分空襲]]では米軍の[[焼夷弾]]により社殿を焼失。[[第二次世界大戦]]後は、[[神道指令]]により[[八幡朝見神社]]に合祀された[[別府市]]青山町の[[温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|温泉神社]]の社殿を移築し、仮殿とした{{cite news |url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337 |title=温泉神社の意外な事実 かつての社殿は春日神社に |newspaper=大分合同新聞 |date=2018-07-23 }}。 現在の社殿は昭和42年（[[1967年]]）に再建されたもので、同年[[10月18日]]に遷座祭が斎行された。なお、社殿の再建後、旧温泉神社の社殿は神輿を納める蔵として使用されている。 昭和43年（[[1968年]]）7月には神社本庁より別表神社に列せられている。 == 社叢 == 社叢は、総面積 26,000m2、樹林面積 15,600m2で、樹木の総数は約500本。「春日神社の森」として、[[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[2月1日]]に大分市の名木（樹林の部）{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html |title=大分市指定名木一覧表 |publisher=大分市 |date=2016-09-14 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}に、また、同年[[3月15日]]に大分県の特別保護樹林に指定されている。 == 祭礼 == * [[1月1日]] - [[歳旦祭]]・[[初詣]] * [[初午|2月初午]] - 摂社稲荷神社初午祭 * [[2月11日]] - [[紀元節|紀元祭]] * [[2月17日]] - [[祈年祭]] * [[4月12日]] - 春季大祭宵祭 * [[4月13日]] - 例祭 * [[4月29日]] - 昭和祭 * [[5月1日]] - 摂社金比羅社祭 * [[6月30日]] - 夏越[[大祓|大祓式]]・[[大祓#茅の輪くぐり|茅の輪神事]] * [[7月18日]] - 夏季大祭宵祭 * [[7月19日]] - 夏越大祭・神輿巡行 * [[9月25日]] - 摂社天満社祭 * [[10月18日]] - 秋季大祭宵祭 * [[10月19日]] - 秋季大祭・潮掻き神事 * [[11月15日]] - [[七五三|七五三祭]] * [[11月23日]] - [[新嘗祭]] * [[12月23日]] - [[天皇誕生日|天長祭]] * [[12月31日]] - 師走大祓式・除夜祭 == 交通 == * 車 - [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[大分駅]]から約5分。 * バス ** 大分駅前7番のりばから、[[大分県立図書館|県立図書館]]行きで「春日神社前」停留所下車。 ** 大分駅前2番のりばから、別府・国東方面行きで「春日浦」停留所下車、徒歩3-4分{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143 |title=春日神社 |publisher=一般財団法人大分市観光協会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp 春日神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや おおいたし}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分市の建築物]] [[Category:大分市の歴史]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

「Oita_kasuga_jinjya.JPG」 を 「Oita_kasuga_jinja.jpg」 に差し替え（[[commons:User:CommonsDelinker|CommonsDelinker]]による。理由：[[:c:COM:FR|File renamed]]: [[:c:COM:FR#FR3|Criterion 3]] (obvious error) · standardizing romanization）

It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant his..."

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Oita kasuga jinja.jpg }} {{Shrine | Name = Kasuga Shrine | Image = [[File:Oita kasuga jinja.jpg|250px]] | Location = 4-6-87 Seike-machi, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Takemikazuchi no Mikoto
Futsunushi no Mikoto
Ame no Koyane no Mikoto
Hime no Ookami | Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | Founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular Festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == Festivals == * January 1: [[Saitansai]] and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode Festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand Festival Eve * April 13: Annual Festival * April 29: Showa Festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine Festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification Ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand Festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand Festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine Festival * October 18: Autumn Grand Festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand Festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] Festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification Ceremony and New Year's Eve Festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasuga Shrine Oita City}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōita City]] [[Category:History of Ōita City]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] == References == revision.comment: 外部リンクの修正 http:// -> https:// (www.pref.oita.jp) ([[Wikipedia:Bot|Bot]]による編集)
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 春日神社 |画像 = [[File:Oita kasuga jinja.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 大分県大分市勢家町4丁目6-87 |位置 = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 武甕槌命
経津主命
天津児屋根命
姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝貞観2年（860年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月13日]]{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/ |title=恒例祭 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }} |神事 = 茅の輪神事（6月30日）
潮掻き神事（10月19日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=春日神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[大分市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]]（たけみかづちのみこと）、[[経津主神|経津主命]]（ふつぬしのみこと）、[[天児屋命|天津児屋根命]]（あまつこやねのみこと）、[[比売神|姫大神]]（ひめおおかみ）の[[春日神|春日四神]]を祀る{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/ |title=由緒 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝や『[[豊後国志]]』によれば、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）、[[豊後国]][[国司]]となった[[藤原世数]]により[[氏神]]である[[大和国]]の[[春日大社]]を[[勧請]]して創建された{{cite encyclopedia |title=春日神社 |author=吉田豊治 |encyclopedia=大分百科事典 |page=137 |editor=大分放送大分百科事典刊行本部 |publisher=大分放送 |date=1980-12-01 }}。『春日神社略記』では、[[天平]]年間（[[729年]] - [[749年]]）、[[国分寺]]建立のために下向した[[石川年足]]が大和[[春日山 (奈良県)|三笠山]]より勧請したとする。また、[[天長]]年間（[[824年]] - [[834年]]）、市川某が大和三笠山より勧請したともされる{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html |title=勢家 春日神社 |publisher=全国春日連合会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 === 中近世 === [[鎌倉時代]]の[[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]）、豊後国[[守護]]となった[[大友氏]]初代当主[[大友能直]]が再興したとされる。一方、『雉城雑誌』は、第2代当主[[大友親秀]]が[[仁治]]3年（[[1243年]]）に社殿を修復して笠和郷[[大分郡]]に10あった[[郷#日本における郷|郷]]のひとつ。の宗廟とし、社領80貫を寄進したと記している。親秀が寄進した菖蒲田等の社領は、近年まで勢家町及び春日町の小字に名を残していた{{Cite book |和書 |title=デジタルブック版「大分今昔」第十五章 春日浦かいわい |author=渡辺克己 |editor=大分合同新聞社 |publisher=NAN-NAN事務局 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf |format=PDF |date=2007-11-23 }}。親秀はまた、[[別当寺]]である鷹雄山神宮寺を再興したり、境内に蓬莱山を築いたともされる{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7 |title=特別保護樹林一覧 |publisher=大分県 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。以来、代々の領主により[[府内 (豊後国)|豊後府内]]の総廟として崇敬された。 [[天正]]14年（[[1586年]]）の[[豊薩合戦|島津軍の府内侵攻]]の際に社殿を焼失。[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）に[[府内藩]]初代藩主[[竹中重利]]が再興した。重利は、慶長12年（[[1607年]]）、[[江戸]]からの帰路に[[播磨灘]]で暴風雨に遭ったが、春日神社に加護を祈って事なきを得たので、境内に松の苗10万本を寄進したという。 [[日根野吉明]]は、[[寛永]]11年（[[1634年]]）に府内藩に入ると、春日神社に神宝を寄進するとともに、伝記を蒐集して祭祀を復興。[[正保]]2年（[[1645年]]）には蓬莱山を再建するとともに、[[過去七仏]]の廟を建立した。 === 近現代 === [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]に列格し、[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に県社に昇格した{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf |title=「大分きれい100選事業」受賞作品【建築物・工作物・まちづくり活動部門】平成24年度おおいた景観モデル賞 48.春日神社 |publisher=大分市 |date=2017-04-01 |format=PDF |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 [[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）[[7月17日]]未明の[[大分空襲]]では米軍の[[焼夷弾]]により社殿を焼失。[[第二次世界大戦]]後は、[[神道指令]]により[[八幡朝見神社]]に合祀された[[別府市]]青山町の[[温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|温泉神社]]の社殿を移築し、仮殿とした{{cite news |url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337 |title=温泉神社の意外な事実 かつての社殿は春日神社に |newspaper=大分合同新聞 |date=2018-07-23 }}。 現在の社殿は昭和42年（[[1967年]]）に再建されたもので、同年[[10月18日]]に遷座祭が斎行された。なお、社殿の再建後、旧温泉神社の社殿は神輿を納める蔵として使用されている。 昭和43年（[[1968年]]）7月には神社本庁より別表神社に列せられている。 == 社叢 == 社叢は、総面積 26,000m2、樹林面積 15,600m2で、樹木の総数は約500本。「春日神社の森」として、[[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[2月1日]]に大分市の名木（樹林の部）{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html |title=大分市指定名木一覧表 |publisher=大分市 |date=2016-09-14 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}に、また、同年[[3月15日]]に大分県の特別保護樹林に指定されている。 == 祭礼 == * [[1月1日]] - [[歳旦祭]]・[[初詣]] * [[初午|2月初午]] - 摂社稲荷神社初午祭 * [[2月11日]] - [[紀元節|紀元祭]] * [[2月17日]] - [[祈年祭]] * [[4月12日]] - 春季大祭宵祭 * [[4月13日]] - 例祭 * [[4月29日]] - 昭和祭 * [[5月1日]] - 摂社金比羅社祭 * [[6月30日]] - 夏越[[大祓|大祓式]]・[[大祓#茅の輪くぐり|茅の輪神事]] * [[7月18日]] - 夏季大祭宵祭 * [[7月19日]] - 夏越大祭・神輿巡行 * [[9月25日]] - 摂社天満社祭 * [[10月18日]] - 秋季大祭宵祭 * [[10月19日]] - 秋季大祭・潮掻き神事 * [[11月15日]] - [[七五三|七五三祭]] * [[11月23日]] - [[新嘗祭]] * [[12月23日]] - [[天皇誕生日|天長祭]] * [[12月31日]] - 師走大祓式・除夜祭 == 交通 == * 車 - [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[大分駅]]から約5分。 * バス ** 大分駅前7番のりばから、[[大分県立図書館|県立図書館]]行きで「春日神社前」停留所下車。 ** 大分駅前2番のりばから、別府・国東方面行きで「春日浦」停留所下車、徒歩3-4分{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143 |title=春日神社 |publisher=一般財団法人大分市観光協会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp 春日神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや おおいたし}} }}。 === 中近世 === [[鎌倉時代]]の[[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]）、豊後国[[守護]]となった[[大友氏]]初代当主[[大友能直]]が再興したとされる。一方、『雉城雑誌』は、第2代当主[[大友親秀]]が[[仁治]]3年（[[1243年]]）に社殿を修復して笠和郷[[大分郡]]に10あった[[郷#日本における郷|郷]]のひとつ。の宗廟とし、社領80貫を寄進したと記している。親秀が寄進した菖蒲田等の社領は、近年まで勢家町及び春日町の小字に名を残していた{{Cite book |和書 |title=デジタルブック版「大分今昔」第十五章 春日浦かいわい |author=渡辺克己 |editor=大分合同新聞社 |publisher=NAN-NAN事務局 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf |format=PDF |date=2007-11-23 }}。親秀はまた、[[別当寺]]である鷹雄山神宮寺を再興したり、境内に蓬莱山を築いたともされる{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7 |title=特別保護樹林一覧 |publisher=大分県 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。以来、代々の領主により[[府内 (豊後国)|豊後府内]]の総廟として崇敬された。 [[天正]]14年（[[1586年]]）の[[豊薩合戦|島津軍の府内侵攻]]の際に社殿を焼失。[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）に[[府内藩]]初代藩主[[竹中重利]]が再興した。重利は、慶長12年（[[1607年]]）、[[江戸]]からの帰路に[[播磨灘]]で暴風雨に遭ったが、春日神社に加護を祈って事なきを得たので、境内に松の苗10万本を寄進したという。 [[日根野吉明]]は、[[寛永]]11年（[[1634年]]）に府内藩に入ると、春日神社に神宝を寄進するとともに、伝記を蒐集して祭祀を復興。[[正保]]2年（[[1645年]]）には蓬莱山を再建するとともに、[[過去七仏]]の廟を建立した。 === 近現代 === [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]に列格し、[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に県社に昇格した{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf |title=「大分きれい100選事業」受賞作品【建築物・工作物・まちづくり活動部門】平成24年度おおいた景観モデル賞 48.春日神社 |publisher=大分市 |date=2017-04-01 |format=PDF |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 [[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）[[7月17日]]未明の[[大分空襲]]では米軍の[[焼夷弾]]により社殿を焼失。[[第二次世界大戦]]後は、[[神道指令]]により[[八幡朝見神社]]に合祀された[[別府市]]青山町の[[温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|温泉神社]]の社殿を移築し、仮殿とした{{cite news |url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337 |title=温泉神社の意外な事実 かつての社殿は春日神社に |newspaper=大分合同新聞 |date=2018-07-23 }}。 現在の社殿は昭和42年（[[1967年]]）に再建されたもので、同年[[10月18日]]に遷座祭が斎行された。なお、社殿の再建後、旧温泉神社の社殿は神輿を納める蔵として使用されている。 昭和43年（[[1968年]]）7月には神社本庁より別表神社に列せられている。 == 社叢 == 社叢は、総面積 26,000m2、樹林面積 15,600m2で、樹木の総数は約500本。「春日神社の森」として、[[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[2月1日]]に大分市の名木（樹林の部）{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html |title=大分市指定名木一覧表 |publisher=大分市 |date=2016-09-14 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}に、また、同年[[3月15日]]に大分県の特別保護樹林に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/attachment/100227.pdf|title=大分県環境白書|page=44|publisher=大分県|accessdate=2024-09-02}}。 == 祭礼 == * [[1月1日]] - [[歳旦祭]]・[[初詣]] * [[初午|2月初午]] - 摂社稲荷神社初午祭 * [[2月11日]] - [[紀元節|紀元祭]] * [[2月17日]] - [[祈年祭]] * [[4月12日]] - 春季大祭宵祭 * [[4月13日]] - 例祭 * [[4月29日]] - 昭和祭 * [[5月1日]] - 摂社金比羅社祭 * [[6月30日]] - 夏越[[大祓|大祓式]]・[[大祓#茅の輪くぐり|茅の輪神事]] * [[7月18日]] - 夏季大祭宵祭 * [[7月19日]] - 夏越大祭・神輿巡行 * [[9月25日]] - 摂社天満社祭 * [[10月18日]] - 秋季大祭宵祭 * [[10月19日]] - 秋季大祭・潮掻き神事 * [[11月15日]] - [[七五三|七五三祭]] * [[11月23日]] - [[新嘗祭]] * [[12月23日]] - [[天皇誕生日|天長祭]] * [[12月31日]] - 師走大祓式・除夜祭 == 交通 == * 車 - [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[大分駅]]から約5分。 * バス ** 大分駅前7番のりばから、[[大分県立図書館|県立図書館]]行きで「春日神社前」停留所下車。 ** 大分駅前2番のりばから、別府・国東方面行きで「春日浦」停留所下車、徒歩3-4分{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143 |title=春日神社 |publisher=一般財団法人大分市観光協会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp 春日神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや おおいたし}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分市の建築物]] [[Category:大分市の歴史]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Oita kasuga jinja.jpg }} {{Shrine | Name = Kasuga Shrine | Image = [[File:Oita kasuga jinja.jpg|250px]] | Location = 4-6-87 Seike-machi, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Takemikazuchi no Mikoto
Futsunushi no Mikoto
Ame no Koyane no Mikoto
Hime no Ookami | Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | Founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular Festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == Festivals == * January 1: [[Saitansai]] and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode Festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand Festival Eve * April 13: Annual Festival * April 29: Showa Festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine Festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification Ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand Festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand Festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine Festival * October 18: Autumn Grand Festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand Festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] Festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification Ceremony and New Year's Eve Festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasuga Shrine Oita City}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōita City]] [[Category:History of Ōita City]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]][[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Oi]] [[Category:Shinto srines in]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[simple:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] }} Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. {{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Oita kasuga jinja.jpg }} {{Shrine | Name = Kasuga Shrine | Image = [[File:Oita kasuga jinja.jpg|250px]] | Location = 4-6-87 Seike-machi, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Takemikazuchi no Mikoto
Futsunushi no Mikoto
Ame no Koyane no Mikoto
Hime no Ookami | Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | Founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular Festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == Festivals == * January 1: [[Saitansai]] and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode Festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand Festival Eve * April 13: Annual Festival * April 29: Showa Festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine Festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification Ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand Festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand Festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine Festival * October 18: Autumn Grand Festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand Festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] Festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification Ceremony and New Year's Eve Festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasuga Shrine Oita City}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōita City]] [[Category:History of Ōita City]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Oi]] [[Category:Shinto srines in]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[simple:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] }} [[ja:春日神社 (大分市)]] {{Kasuga Faith}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Hime no Ookami | Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | Founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate Names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular Festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == Festivals == * January 1: [[Saitan Festival|Saitansai]] and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode Festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand Festival Eve * April 13: Annual Festival * April 29: Showa Festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine Festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification Ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand Festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand Festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine Festival * October 18: Autumn Grand Festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand Festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] Festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification Ceremony and New Year's Eve Festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasuga Shrine Oita City}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == Festivals == * January 1: [[Saitan Festival|Saitansai]] and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode Festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand Festival Eve * April 13: Annual Festival * April 29: Showa Festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine Festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification Ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand Festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand Festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine Festival * October 18: Autumn Grand Festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand Festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] Festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification Ceremony and New Year's Eve Festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasuga Shrine Oita City}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōita City]] [[Category:History of Ōita City]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Oi]] [[Category:Shinto srines in]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[simple:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] }} [[ja:春日神社 (大分市)]] {{Kasuga Faith}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Hime no Ookami | rank = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate names = | Pilgrimage = | annual festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: [[Saitan festival|Saitansai]] and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: annual festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasuga Shrine Oita City}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōita City]] [[Category:History of Ōita City]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| Hime no Ookami | rank = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate names = | Pilgrimage = | annual festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: [[Saitan festival|Saitansai]] and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: annual festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasuga Shrine Oita City}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōita City]] [[Category:History of Ōita City]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Oi]] [[Category:Shinto srines in]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[simple:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] }} [[ja:春日神社 (大分市)]] {{Kasuga Faith}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}
