revision.comment: 29 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:春日神社 (大分市) up to rev 103113718
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 春日神社 |画像 = [[File:Oita kasuga jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[大分県]][[大分市]]勢家町4丁目6-87 |位置 = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[武甕槌命]]
[[経津主命]]
[[天児屋命|天津児屋根命]]
[[姫大神]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建 = 伝[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月13日]]{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/ |title=恒例祭 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }} |神事 = 茅の輪神事（6月30日）
潮掻き神事（10月19日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=春日神社|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[大分市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]]（たけみかづちのみこと）、[[経津主神|経津主命]]（ふつぬしのみこと）、[[天児屋命|天津児屋根命]]（あまつこやねのみこと）、[[比売神|姫大神]]（ひめおおかみ）の[[春日神|春日四神]]を祀る{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/ |title=由緒 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝や『[[豊後国志]]』によれば、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）、[[豊後国]][[国司]]となった[[藤原世数]]により[[氏神]]である[[大和国]]の[[春日大社]]を[[勧請]]して創建された{{cite encyclopedia |title=春日神社 |author=吉田豊治 |encyclopedia=大分百科事典 |page=137 |editor=大分放送大分百科事典刊行本部 |publisher=大分放送 |date=1980-12-01 }}。『春日神社略記』では、[[天平]]年間（[[729年]] - [[749年]]）、[[国分寺]]建立のために下向した[[石川年足]]が大和[[春日山 (奈良県)|三笠山]]より勧請したとする。また、[[天長]]年間（[[824年]] - [[834年]]）、市川某が大和三笠山より勧請したともされる{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html |title=勢家 春日神社 |publisher=全国春日連合会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 === 中近世 === [[鎌倉時代]]の[[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]）、豊後国[[守護]]となった[[大友氏]]初代当主[[大友能直]]が再興したとされる。一方、『雉城雑誌』は、第2代当主[[大友親秀]]が[[仁治]]3年（[[1243年]]）に社殿を修復して笠和郷[[大分郡]]に10あった[[郷#日本における郷|郷]]のひとつ。の宗廟とし、社領80貫を寄進したと記している。親秀が寄進した菖蒲田等の社領は、近年まで勢家町及び春日町の小字に名を残していた{{Cite book |和書 |title=デジタルブック版「大分今昔」第十五章 春日浦かいわい |author=渡辺克己 |editor=大分合同新聞社 |publisher=NAN-NAN事務局 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf |format=PDF |date=2007-11-23 }}。親秀はまた、[[別当寺]]である鷹雄山神宮寺を再興したり、境内に蓬莱山を築いたともされる{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7 |title=特別保護樹林一覧 |publisher=大分県 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。以来、代々の領主により[[府内 (豊後国)|豊後府内]]の総廟として崇敬された。 [[天正]]14年（[[1586年]]）の[[豊薩合戦|島津軍の府内侵攻]]の際に社殿を焼失。[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）に[[府内藩]]初代藩主[[竹中重利]]が再興した。重利は、慶長12年（[[1607年]]）、[[江戸]]からの帰路に[[播磨灘]]で暴風雨に遭ったが、春日神社に加護を祈って事なきを得たので、境内に松の苗10万本を寄進したという。 [[日根野吉明]]は、[[寛永]]11年（[[1634年]]）に府内藩に入ると、春日神社に神宝を寄進するとともに、伝記を蒐集して祭祀を復興。[[正保]]2年（[[1645年]]）には蓬莱山を再建するとともに、[[過去七仏]]の廟を建立した。 === 近現代 === [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]に列格し、[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に県社に昇格した{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf |title=「大分きれい100選事業」受賞作品【建築物・工作物・まちづくり活動部門】平成24年度おおいた景観モデル賞 48.春日神社 |publisher=大分市 |date=2017-04-01 |format=PDF |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 [[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）[[7月17日]]未明の[[大分空襲]]では米軍の[[焼夷弾]]により社殿を焼失。[[第二次世界大戦]]後は、[[神道指令]]により[[八幡朝見神社]]に合祀された[[別府市]]青山町の[[温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|温泉神社]]の社殿を移築し、仮殿とした{{cite news |url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337 |title=温泉神社の意外な事実 かつての社殿は春日神社に |newspaper=大分合同新聞 |date=2018-07-23 }}。 現在の社殿は昭和42年（[[1967年]]）に再建されたもので、同年[[10月18日]]に遷座祭が斎行された。なお、社殿の再建後、旧温泉神社の社殿は神輿を納める蔵として使用されている。 昭和43年（[[1968年]]）7月には神社本庁より別表神社に列せられている。 == 社叢 == 社叢は、総面積 26,000m2、樹林面積 15,600m2で、樹木の総数は約500本。「春日神社の森」として、[[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[2月1日]]に大分市の名木（樹林の部）{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html |title=大分市指定名木一覧表 |publisher=大分市 |date=2016-09-14 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}に、また、同年[[3月15日]]に大分県の特別保護樹林に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/attachment/100227.pdf|title=大分県環境白書|page=44|publisher=大分県|accessdate=2024-09-02}}。 == 祭礼 == * [[1月1日]] - [[歳旦祭]]・[[初詣]] * [[初午|2月初午]] - 摂社稲荷神社初午祭 * [[2月11日]] - [[紀元節|紀元祭]] * [[2月17日]] - [[祈年祭]] * [[4月12日]] - 春季大祭宵祭 * [[4月13日]] - 例祭 * [[4月29日]] - 昭和祭 * [[5月1日]] - 摂社金比羅社祭 * [[6月30日]] - 夏越[[大祓|大祓式]]・[[大祓#茅の輪くぐり|茅の輪神事]] * [[7月18日]] - 夏季大祭宵祭 * [[7月19日]] - 夏越大祭・神輿巡行 * [[9月25日]] - 摂社天満社祭 * [[10月18日]] - 秋季大祭宵祭 * [[10月19日]] - 秋季大祭・潮掻き神事 * [[11月15日]] - [[七五三|七五三祭]] * [[11月23日]] - [[新嘗祭]] * [[12月23日]] - [[天皇誕生日|天長祭]] * [[12月31日]] - 師走大祓式・除夜祭 == 交通 == * 車 - [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[大分駅]]から約5分。 * バス ** 大分駅前7番のりばから、[[大分県立図書館|県立図書館]]行きで「春日神社前」停留所下車。 ** 大分駅前2番のりばから、別府・国東方面行きで「春日浦」停留所下車、徒歩3-4分{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143 |title=春日神社 |publisher=一般財団法人大分市観光協会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == [[Category:別表神社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[春日神社 (大分市)]] to [[Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 春日神社 |画像 = [[File:Oita kasuga jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[大分県]][[大分市]]勢家町4丁目6-87 |位置 = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[武甕槌命]]
[[経津主命]]
[[天児屋命|天津児屋根命]]
[[姫大神]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建 = 伝[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月13日]]{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/ |title=恒例祭 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }} |神事 = 茅の輪神事（6月30日）
潮掻き神事（10月19日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=春日神社|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[大分市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]]（たけみかづちのみこと）、[[経津主神|経津主命]]（ふつぬしのみこと）、[[天児屋命|天津児屋根命]]（あまつこやねのみこと）、[[比売神|姫大神]]（ひめおおかみ）の[[春日神|春日四神]]を祀る{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/ |title=由緒 |publisher=春日神社 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝や『[[豊後国志]]』によれば、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）、[[豊後国]][[国司]]となった[[藤原世数]]により[[氏神]]である[[大和国]]の[[春日大社]]を[[勧請]]して創建された{{cite encyclopedia |title=春日神社 |author=吉田豊治 |encyclopedia=大分百科事典 |page=137 |editor=大分放送大分百科事典刊行本部 |publisher=大分放送 |date=1980-12-01 }}。『春日神社略記』では、[[天平]]年間（[[729年]] - [[749年]]）、[[国分寺]]建立のために下向した[[石川年足]]が大和[[春日山 (奈良県)|三笠山]]より勧請したとする。また、[[天長]]年間（[[824年]] - [[834年]]）、市川某が大和三笠山より勧請したともされる{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html |title=勢家 春日神社 |publisher=全国春日連合会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 === 中近世 === [[鎌倉時代]]の[[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]）、豊後国[[守護]]となった[[大友氏]]初代当主[[大友能直]]が再興したとされる。一方、『雉城雑誌』は、第2代当主[[大友親秀]]が[[仁治]]3年（[[1243年]]）に社殿を修復して笠和郷[[大分郡]]に10あった[[郷#日本における郷|郷]]のひとつ。の宗廟とし、社領80貫を寄進したと記している。親秀が寄進した菖蒲田等の社領は、近年まで勢家町及び春日町の小字に名を残していた{{Cite book |和書 |title=デジタルブック版「大分今昔」第十五章 春日浦かいわい |author=渡辺克己 |editor=大分合同新聞社 |publisher=NAN-NAN事務局 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf |format=PDF |date=2007-11-23 }}。親秀はまた、[[別当寺]]である鷹雄山神宮寺を再興したり、境内に蓬莱山を築いたともされる{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7 |title=特別保護樹林一覧 |publisher=大分県 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。以来、代々の領主により[[府内 (豊後国)|豊後府内]]の総廟として崇敬された。 [[天正]]14年（[[1586年]]）の[[豊薩合戦|島津軍の府内侵攻]]の際に社殿を焼失。[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）に[[府内藩]]初代藩主[[竹中重利]]が再興した。重利は、慶長12年（[[1607年]]）、[[江戸]]からの帰路に[[播磨灘]]で暴風雨に遭ったが、春日神社に加護を祈って事なきを得たので、境内に松の苗10万本を寄進したという。 [[日根野吉明]]は、[[寛永]]11年（[[1634年]]）に府内藩に入ると、春日神社に神宝を寄進するとともに、伝記を蒐集して祭祀を復興。[[正保]]2年（[[1645年]]）には蓬莱山を再建するとともに、[[過去七仏]]の廟を建立した。 === 近現代 === [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]に列格し、[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に県社に昇格した{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf |title=「大分きれい100選事業」受賞作品【建築物・工作物・まちづくり活動部門】平成24年度おおいた景観モデル賞 48.春日神社 |publisher=大分市 |date=2017-04-01 |format=PDF |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 [[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）[[7月17日]]未明の[[大分空襲]]では米軍の[[焼夷弾]]により社殿を焼失。[[第二次世界大戦]]後は、[[神道指令]]により[[八幡朝見神社]]に合祀された[[別府市]]青山町の[[温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|温泉神社]]の社殿を移築し、仮殿とした{{cite news |url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337 |title=温泉神社の意外な事実 かつての社殿は春日神社に |newspaper=大分合同新聞 |date=2018-07-23 }}。 現在の社殿は昭和42年（[[1967年]]）に再建されたもので、同年[[10月18日]]に遷座祭が斎行された。なお、社殿の再建後、旧温泉神社の社殿は神輿を納める蔵として使用されている。 昭和43年（[[1968年]]）7月には神社本庁より別表神社に列せられている。 == 社叢 == 社叢は、総面積 26,000m2、樹林面積 15,600m2で、樹木の総数は約500本。「春日神社の森」として、[[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[2月1日]]に大分市の名木（樹林の部）{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html |title=大分市指定名木一覧表 |publisher=大分市 |date=2016-09-14 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}に、また、同年[[3月15日]]に大分県の特別保護樹林に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/attachment/100227.pdf|title=大分県環境白書|page=44|publisher=大分県|accessdate=2024-09-02}}。 == 祭礼 == * [[1月1日]] - [[歳旦祭]]・[[初詣]] * [[初午|2月初午]] - 摂社稲荷神社初午祭 * [[2月11日]] - [[紀元節|紀元祭]] * [[2月17日]] - [[祈年祭]] * [[4月12日]] - 春季大祭宵祭 * [[4月13日]] - 例祭 * [[4月29日]] - 昭和祭 * [[5月1日]] - 摂社金比羅社祭 * [[6月30日]] - 夏越[[大祓|大祓式]]・[[大祓#茅の輪くぐり|茅の輪神事]] * [[7月18日]] - 夏季大祭宵祭 * [[7月19日]] - 夏越大祭・神輿巡行 * [[9月25日]] - 摂社天満社祭 * [[10月18日]] - 秋季大祭宵祭 * [[10月19日]] - 秋季大祭・潮掻き神事 * [[11月15日]] - [[七五三|七五三祭]] * [[11月23日]] - [[新嘗祭]] * [[12月23日]] - [[天皇誕生日|天長祭]] * [[12月31日]] - 師走大祓式・除夜祭 == 交通 == * 車 - [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[大分駅]]から約5分。 * バス ** 大分駅前7番のりばから、[[大分県立図書館|県立図書館]]行きで「春日神社前」停留所下車。 ** 大分駅前2番のりばから、別府・国東方面行きで「春日浦」停留所下車、徒歩3-4分{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143 |title=春日神社 |publisher=一般財団法人大分市観光協会 |accessdate=2018-07-23 }}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp 春日神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや おおいたし}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分市の建築物]] [[Category:大分市の歴史]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{afc comment|1=source:[[every:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:47, 13 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Oita kasuga jinja.jpg |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kasuga Shrine | image = [[File:Oita kasuga jinja.jpg|250px]] | location = 4-6-87 Seike-machi, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Takemikazuchi no Mikoto
Futsunushi no Mikoto
Ame no Koyane no Mikoto
Hime no Ookami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate names = | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was Ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saiten-sai}} and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[simple:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| Shrine (Ōita)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasuga Shrine Oita City}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (大分市)|version=103113718|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Futsunushi no Mikoto
Ame no Koyane no Mikoto
Hime no Ookami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate names = | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was Ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saiten-sai|12=draft}} and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[simple:Kasuga It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Oita kasuga jinja.jpg |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kasuga Shrine | image = [[File:Oita kasuga jinja.jpg|250px]] | location = 4-6-87 Seike-machi, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Takemikazuchi no Mikoto
Futsunushi no Mikoto
Ame no Koyane no Mikoto
Hime no Ookami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate names = | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was Ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saiten-sai|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Beppyo revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Source Everybodywiki]] to [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=source:[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:47, 13 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Oita kasuga jinja.jpg |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kasuga Shrine | image = [[File:Oita kasuga jinja.jpg|250px]] | location = 4-6-87 Seike-machi, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Takemikazuchi no Mikoto
Futsunushi no Mikoto
Ame no Koyane no Mikoto
Hime no Ookami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate names = | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was Ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saiten-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (大分市)|version=103113718|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasuga Shrine Oita City}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)}} {{Draft categories| [[simple:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] }} [[every:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] [[simple:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:春日神社 (大分市)]] [[ja:Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was Ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saiten-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (大分市)|version=103113718|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[simple:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] }} [[every:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] [[simple:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:春日神社 (大分市)]] [[ja:Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

Futsunushi no Mikoto
Ame no Koyane no Mikoto
Hime no Ookami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate names = | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame no Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was Ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saiten-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was Ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saiten-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=source:[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:47, 13 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Oita kasuga jinja.jpg |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kasuga Shrine | image = [[File:Oita kasuga jinja.jpg|250px]] | location = 4-6-87 Seike-machi, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Takemikazuchi no Mikoto
Futsunushi no Mikoto
Ame no Koyane no Mikoto
Hime no Ookami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate names = | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was Ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saiten-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated import successful]]

The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saiten-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:大分市の建築物
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=source:[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:47, 13 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Oita kasuga jinja.jpg |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kasuga Shrine | image = [[File:Oita kasuga jinja.jpg|250px]] | location = 4-6-87 Seike-machi, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|14|42.5|N|131|35|53.5|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Takemikazuchi no Mikoto
Futsunushi no Mikoto
Ame no Koyane no Mikoto
Hime no Ookami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | founded = Traditional date: Jōgan 2 (860) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate names = | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[April 13]]{{Cite web|title=Regular festivals|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/event/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}} | Rituals = Chino-wa Shinji (June 30)
Shiokaki Shinji (October 19) | map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Kasuga Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōita City]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine and is now listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == Kasuga Shrine enshrines the four deities of [[Kasuga]]: * [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame no Koyane no Mikoto]] * [[Himegami|Hime no Ookami]] == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition and the ''[[Bungo no Kuni-shi]]'', the shrine was founded in Jōgan 2 (860) by [[Fujiwara no Yotsuke]], a [[kokushi]] (provincial governor) of [[Bungo Province]], who enshrined the deities of the [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] from [[Yamato Province]] as his clan's guardian deities{{Cite web|title=History|url=http://www.kasuganomori.jp/history/|publisher=Kasuga Shrine|accessdate=2018-07-23}}{{cite encyclopedia|title=Kasuga Shrine|author=Yoshida Toyoharu|encyclopedia=Ōita Encyclopedia|page=137|editor=Ōita Broadcasting System Ōita Encyclopedia Editorial Office|publisher=Ōita Broadcasting System|date=1980-12-01}}. Another tradition in the ''Kasuga Shrine Abbreviation'' states that Ishikawa Toshitane enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain in Yamato during the [[Tenpyo]] era (729-749). Additionally, it is said that Ichikawa某 enshrined the deities from Mikasa Mountain during the [[Tencho]] era (824-834){{Cite web|title=Seike Kasuga Shrine|url=http://kasugarengou.jp/list/2016/03/post-386.html|publisher=National Kasuga Union|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. === Medieval to Early Modern Period === In Kenkyu 7 (1196), the first lord of the [[Otomo clan]], [[Otomo Yoshiaki]], is said to have rebuilt the shrine. However, ''Kichijo Magazine'' states that the second lord, [[Otomo Yoshihide]], repaired the shrine in Ninji 3 (1243) and dedicated 80 koku of rice fields. The names of the rice fields dedicated by Yoshihide were retained in the local place names until recently{{Cite book|title=Digital Book "Ōita Past and Present" Chapter 15: Around Kasugaura|author=Watanabe Katsumi|editor=Ōita Godo Shimbun|publisher=NAN-NAN Office|url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita015a1.pdf|format=PDF|date=2007-11-23}}. Yoshihide also revived the affiliated temple Takaoyama Jinguji and is said to have built Mt. Horai on the grounds{{Cite web|title=Specially Protected Forest List|url=http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/16210/hogojurin.html#7|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. Since then, it has been revered as the general shrine of [[Bungo Fuchu]] by successive lords. In Tensho 14 (1586), the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Invasion of Bungo|Satsuma invasion of Bungo]]. They were rebuilt in Keicho 8 (1603) by the first lord of the [[Funai Domain]], [[Takenaka Shigeyoshi]]. According to tradition, Shigeyoshi offered 100,000 pine seedlings to the shrine after surviving a storm on the Harima Sea while returning from [[Edo]] in Keicho 12 (1607). [[Hineno Yoshiaki]] revived the shrine's rituals and donated sacred treasures upon his arrival in Funai Domain in Kan'ei 11 (1634). In Shoho 2 (1645), he rebuilt Mt. Horai and constructed a shrine for the Seven Past Buddhas. === Modern Period === In Meiji 4 (1871), the shrine was Ranked as a Gōsha, and in Taisho 6 (1917), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title="Ōita Beautiful 100" Award-Winning Projects in the Architecture, Structures, and Town Development Activities Category, 24th Oita Landscape Model Award, 48. Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o170/machizukuri/toshi/documents/5146717c002.pdf|publisher=Ōita City|date=2017-04-01|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. On July 17, 1945, the shrine buildings were destroyed by incendiary bombs during the [[Bombing of Ōita]]. After World War II, the shrine buildings from the [[Onsen Shrines|Onsen Shrine]] in [[Beppu]] were relocated to serve as temporary structures{{cite news|title=Surprising Facts about Onsen Shrine: The Former Shrine Buildings are at Kasuga Shrine|url=http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2018/07/20/JD0057123337|newspaper=Ōita Godo Shimbun|date=2018-07-23}}. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in Showa 42 (1967), with the relocation ceremony held on October 18. The old Onsen Shrine buildings are now used as a storage facility for portable shrines. In July 1968, the shrine was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Shrine Forest == The shrine forest covers a total area of 26,000 m², with a wooded area of 15,600 m² and approximately 500 trees. Designated as a "Notable Tree Forest" by Ōita City on February 1, 1974{{Cite web|title=List of Notable Trees in Ōita City|url=http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o177/machizukuri/toshi/1438768127009.html|publisher=Ōita City|date=2016-09-14|accessdate=2018-07-23}}, it was also designated as a special protected forest by Ōita Prefecture on March 15, 1974. == festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saiten-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and [[Hatsumode]] * Early February: Inari Shrine Hatsumode festival * February 11: [[Kigensetsu|Kigensai]] * February 17: [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai]] * April 12: Spring Grand festival Eve * April 13: festival * April 29: Showa festival * May 1: Kinpira Shrine festival * June 30: Summer Great Purification ceremony and [[Chino-wa Shinji]] * July 18: Summer Grand festival Eve * July 19: Summer Grand festival and Portable Shrine Procession * September 25: Tenmangu Shrine festival * October 18: Autumn Grand festival Eve * October 19: Autumn Grand festival and Shiokaki Shinji * November 15: [[Shichi-Go-San]] festival * November 23: [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * December 23: Tencho-sai * December 31: Great Purification ceremony and New Year's Eve festival == Access == * By car: About 5 minutes from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Oita Station]]. * By bus: ** From Oita Station, take a bus bound for [[Oita Prefectural Library]] from Bus Stop No. 7 and get off at "Kasuga Shrine-mae". ** From Bus Stop No. 2 at Oita Station, take a bus bound for Beppu/Kunisaki, get off at "Kasugaura", and walk for 3-4 minutes{{Cite web|title=Kasuga Shrine|url=https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/sp/spots/detail/1143|publisher=Oita City Tourism Association|accessdate=2018-07-23}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kasuganomori.jp Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (大分市)|version=103113718|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[simple:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] }} [[every:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] [[simple:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:春日神社 (大分市)]] [[ja:Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Ōita)]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:大分市の建築物]]
